title: Mildly Smoky Egyptian Pepper Tagine
description: A garlicky and crumbly one-pot Egyptian dish featuring roasted peppers, tempered chocolate, and ovo-vegetarian friendly ingredients, this tagine is a delicious and unique meal option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place the red bell peppers on a baking sheet and roast for 30 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
Remove the peppers from the oven and let them cool, then peel off the skin and slice into strips.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the minced garlic, cooking for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
In a small bowl, melt the dark chocolate in the microwave in 10 second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Temper the chocolate by stirring in a small amount of the warm garlic and oil mixture, then set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt, then stir in the crumbled feta cheese.
Add the roasted peppers to the bowl and stir to combine with the egg mixture.
Pour the egg and pepper mixture into the skillet with the garlic and oil, and cook over medium heat until the eggs are set.
Drizzle the tempered chocolate over the top of the tagine and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
